question: What codegen/runtime capability closes the ctx-growing attention tail, given bounded Route B/B5 is exhausted?

why_route_b_did_not_clear_wd.mechanism: COMBINE OVERLAP / off-critical-path (measured 3-point curve: combine 9.7->5.8->4.0us moved whole-decode +5.41->+5.71->+5.66%, i.e. 2.4x cheaper combine = +0.25%, further cut = ~0). The Amdahl/combine-tax projection was REFUTED.
why_route_b_did_not_clear_wd.note: ceiling is the ceiling of the SEPARATE tile+combine form; partly structural (attention overlaps weight-GEMV) and partly an artifact of the part/meta HBM round-trip between tile and combine

inexpressible_object: a SINGLE kernel that is BOTH LDS-tiled v_dot2 (q.k + PV) AND carries register-resident online-softmax (m,l,acc) state across the KV loop. tinygrad has two MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE paths: tiled-GEMM (concrete reduce dim, separate kernels, no LDS/v_dot2 at decode shape) and .set/.after register fusion (one kernel but SCALAR, 0 v_dot2, 0 LDS). llama's tile is both at once.

llama_oracle_advantage.unit: x faster standalone (pure GPU) vs gqa_coop_vec
llama_oracle_advantage.what_llama_does: LDS-staged K/V (ds_load_b128), dense v_dot2_f32_f16 (256-1024/kernel), online softmax+PV in ONE kernel; tinygrad splits into 6-8 scalar HBM-roundtrip kernels (flash_partial = 0 v_dot2, 0 LDS, 201 GFLOPS latency-bound)

A_native_linearizer_coupled_multireduce.prior_failure: expressiveness NOT the wall (coupled (m,l,acc) kernel runs correct today) -- the .set/.after idiom emits SCALAR code; tiled-GEMM and .set/.after are mutually exclusive; symbolic-count tiled batched matmul inexpressible (matmul-pv eval-fail)
A_native_linearizer_coupled_multireduce.risk: UNBOUNDED core tinygrad (codegen/* renderer/*, multi-week, all-backend blast radius)

B_amdgcn_single_fused_tile.target: ONE owned AMDGCN kernel = QK+online-softmax+PV+v_dot2+LDS with cross-split combine FOLDED IN (no part/meta HBM round-trip)
B_amdgcn_single_fused_tile.prior_failure: NOT YET TRIED -- B3/B4/B5 always emitted SEPARATE tile->combine (two graph nodes); the tile body already has v_dot2 (__builtin_amdgcn_fdot2)+LDS+online softmax
B_amdgcn_single_fused_tile.different_now: fold combine into owned_flash_tile_gqa -> removes the serial part/meta round-trip the +5.7% ceiling never tested
B_amdgcn_single_fused_tile.first_gate: single fused tile, default-off graph node, byte-correct vs gqa_coop_vec, local A/B >=1.5x vs gqa_coop_vec, W==D projection
B_amdgcn_single_fused_tile.risk: BOUNDED fork-local hand-asm; HCQ bridge proven

C_renderer_template.target: renderer primitive/schedule-template for flash decode (packed-fp16 dot + LDS-tile idiom + fused-reduction scheduling)
C_renderer_template.risk: UNBOUNDED core codegen, highest blast radius

D_improve_split_attention.prior_failure: combine overlaps -> cheaper combine doesn't transfer (refuted)

E_do_nothing_rest.verdict: premature -- the single fused tile gate is untried

ceiling_exceed_condition: the +5.7%@4096 is the SEPARATE-form ceiling (combine overlaps). A SINGLE fused tile (no part/meta round-trip) is the ONE lever the overlap mechanism does NOT rule out -- plausible but UNPROVEN it clears +7%. Hard stop: if a single fused tile still saturates <=+5.7%@4096 in W==D, the ceiling is structural (attention overlap-bounded share) -> REST_DECODE.
cross_lane_link: the single fused owned tile is an OPAQUE attention read of cache_kv -> it is ALSO the substrate Lane 1 needs to remove the KV copy (the default functional gqa_coop_vec reduce re-hits the read-after-write hazard; an opaque tile does not). Building this tile unlocks BOTH lanes.
